items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Trump indicated a deal within a week. Again.
items.summary: BOJ should hike in June and signal clear rate path, SMFG markets chief says Citi reiterate forecast for a 25bp RBA rate hike at the Bank's August meeting. Nvidia's Huang says supply secured for robust AI growth despite constraints Australia's net trade and flat government spending cloud GDP outlook S&P 500 futures analysis today: ES JUN26 fails repair near 7632 Japan one step from historic yen collapse, SMBC Nikko warns Australia widening current account deficit, inventory draw to weigh on Q1 GDP Ethereum Analysis Today: ETH Repairs From Washout, But Bulls Need Acceptance Above 2020.5 White House trims tariffs on farm and industrial gear, tweaks metals regime PBOC sets USD/ CNY central rate at 6.8187 (vs. estimate at 6.7720) RBA's hawkish Harper hints herald hike Tokyo and Washington closely monitoring forex markets together, finance minister confirms Japan eyes sales tax cut from 2027 but bond market (and yen!) risks loom large RBNZ risks crushing already weakened economy to contain oil-driven inflation Kiwibank warn Commerzbank lifts Brent to $90 on Hormuz closure, no return to pre-war prices South Korea May 2026 inflation data higher than expected, keeps BoK on hike alert Israel PM contradicts Trump, will continue operating against Hezbollah in Southern Lebanon Trump says Hormuz deal is "looking good" but his credibility gap keeps markets on edge BofA contrarian sell signal nears trigger as S&P optimism hits highest since February 2025 Oil's biggest single-day gain in weeks as Middle East tensions flare again ICYMI! Strategy sells Bitcoin for first time since 2022, breaking "never sell" pledge Record close for the major indices once again Key points: Trump told ABC News a Hormuz ceasefire extension and reopening deal could come within a week, but markets gave the remarks little credit given his track record on conflict timelines White House cut tariffs on agricultural equipment including combines and harvesters, a rare Trump concession on trade Australia lifted its minimum wage by 4.75%; RBA board member Harper sounded a hawkish note on inflation, adding to the case for an August hike Australian Q1 data pointed to a soft Wednesday GDP print, with net trade subtracting 0.8 percentage points and government spending flat Japan is pursuing a food sales tax cut from 8% to 1% from April 2027, a move with bond market implications already demonstrated when Takaichi first floated the plan in January Finance Minister Katayama flagged close US-Japan forex coordination; USD/JPY was quiet around 159.70 Alphabet announced an $80 billion equity raise for AI infrastructure, including a $10 billion private placement from Berkshire Hathaway Trump told ABC News he thinks he will have an agreement with Iran to extend the ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz over the next week. Trump is credibility-challenged on this front, however, and his remarks went largely unheeded by markets. Further from the US, and in a welcome climbdown, the White House said it will reduce tariffs on agricultural equipment such as combines and harvesters to ease costs for American farmers and manufacturers. Australia lifted its minimum wage by 4.75%. Separately, Reserve Bank of Australia board member Ian Harper sounded a hawkish note, saying stronger-than-expected domestic demand and the return of capacity constraints have reopened the output gap, prompting markets to price in further policy action. Harper flagged persistent inflation as a genuine concern, noting that market-based inflation measures have moved higher, which he described as worrying. The RBA meets next on June 15 and 16, where a pause is widely expected, but the faster rise in award wages alongside Harper's comments on inflation expectations add to the case for another hike, most likely in August. Australian data today indicated the GDP print due Wednesday is shaping up as a soft one, with net trade subtracting 0.8 percentage points, well beyond the 0.5 points forecast, and government spending contributing nothing. Inventories adding 0.2 points provides only partial offset, leaving household consumption and business investment to carry the quarter. Japanese media reported that Japan's government is pursuing a food sales tax cut from 8% to 1% from April 2027 for two years. The proposal carries bond market implications that have already been road-tested: Prime Minister Takaichi's initial announcement in January caused a spike in yields as investors priced in further deterioration of Japan's already stretched fiscal position. A confirmed rollout would likely revive that pressure. Finance Minister Katayama subsequently weighed in with intervention warnings, confirming that Japan is closely coordinating with the United States on foreign exchange, with both sides actively monitoring conditions. USD/JPY did little on the session, sitting around 159.70, with the dollar quiet more broadly. It was a subdued day across markets generally, with South Korean and Japanese equities pulling back from recent record highs. On the corporate front, Alphabet announced a proposed $80 billion equity capital raise to expand AI infrastructure and compute capacity, comprising a $30 billion underwritten offering, a $40 billion at-the-market programme, and a $10 billion private placement from Berkshire Hathaway. -- Australia's cash rate, inflation rate, and the wage hike above both: This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: SMFG市场主管表示，日本央行应于6月加息并明确利率路径；花旗重申预测，澳大利亚央行将在8月会议上加息25个基点。英伟达CEO黄仁勋表示，尽管面临供应限制，但已确保充足供应以支持强劲的AI增长 澳大利亚贸易顺差收窄及政府支出持平令GDP前景蒙上阴影 今日标普500指数期货分析：ES JUN26合约在7632点附近未能成功反弹 三菱日联证券警告：日元距离历史性崩盘仅一步之遥 澳大利亚经常账户赤字扩大，库存减少将拖累一季度GDP 今日以太坊分析： ETH自暴跌中反弹，但多头需突破2020.5点才能确立趋势 白宫下调农产品和工业设备关税，调整金属关税政策 中国人民银行将美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8187（较 （预计为6.7720）澳洲联储鹰派成员哈珀暗示即将加息 东京和华盛顿正密切共同监控外汇市场，财政部长证实 日本计划自2027年起下调消费税，但债券市场（及日元！）风险依然严峻 新西兰联储为抑制石油驱动的通胀，或将重创本已疲软的经济 基维银行发出警告 德国商业银行因霍尔木兹海峡封锁将布伦特原油价格上调至90美元，油价不会回落至战前 韩国2026年5月通胀数据高于预期，韩国央行保持加息警惕 以色列总理与特朗普意见相左，将继续在黎巴嫩南部对真主党采取行动 特朗普称霍尔木兹协议“进展顺利”，但其公信力缺失令市场持续紧张 随着标普乐观情绪升至2025年2月以来最高，美银逆势卖出信号临近触发 受中东局势再度紧张影响，油价创数周来最大单日涨幅 错过了吗！Strategy自2022年以来首次抛售比特币，打破“永不卖出”承诺 主要股指再创收盘新高 要点：特朗普告诉美国广播公司新闻，霍尔木兹海峡停火延期和重开协议可能在一周内达成，但鉴于他在冲突时间表上的记录，市场对其言论几乎不予采信 白宫下调了农业设备的关税 包括联合收割机在内，这是特朗普在贸易问题上罕见的让步；澳大利亚将最低工资上调了4.75%；澳大利亚储备银行（RBA）董事会成员哈珀就通胀问题发表了鹰派言论，进一步强化了8月加息的可能性；澳大利亚第一季度数据显示周三公布的GDP数据可能疲软，其中贸易净额拖累增长0.8个百分点，政府支出持平；日本正计划从2027年4月起将食品销售税从8%下调至1%， 这一举措对债券市场的影响已在1月高市早苗首次提出该计划时显现；财务大臣片山刚表示将加强日美外汇协调； 美元兑日元汇率在159.70附近维持平稳。Alphabet宣布将通过800亿美元的股权融资用于人工智能基础设施建设，其中包括伯克希尔·哈撒韦公司100亿美元的私募配售。特朗普告诉美国广播公司新闻，他认为将在未来一周内与伊朗达成协议，以延长停火并重新开放霍尔木兹海峡。然而，特朗普在此问题上的可信度备受质疑，市场对其言论基本不予理会。 此外，在美国方面，白宫做出了一项值得欢迎的让步，表示将降低联合收割机等农业设备的关税，以减轻美国农民和制造商的成本负担。澳大利亚将最低工资上调了4.75%。此外，澳大利亚储备银行（RBA）董事会成员伊恩·哈珀（Ian Harper）释放了鹰派信号，称强于预期的国内需求以及产能瓶颈的重新出现，导致产出缺口再度扩大，促使市场预期将采取进一步的政策行动。哈珀指出，持续的通胀是一个真正的担忧，并指出基于市场的通胀指标已有所上升，他称这令人担忧。澳联储下次会议定于6月15日至16日举行，市场普遍预期将暂停加息，但集体协议工资的加速上涨，加上哈珀关于通胀预期的言论，为再次加息提供了更多依据，最有可能在8月实施。今日澳大利亚数据显示，定于周三公布的GDP数据可能表现疲软，其中净贸易拖累增长0.8个百分点，远超0.5个百分点的预期，而政府支出则未作贡献。 库存增加0.2个百分点仅起到部分抵消作用，本季度的经济增长主要仍依赖于家庭消费和企业投资。日本媒体报道称，日本政府正计划从2027年4月起，将食品销售税从8%下调至1%，为期两年。该提议对债券市场的影响此前已得到验证：高市首相今年1月的初步宣布曾导致收益率飙升，因为投资者预期日本本已捉襟见肘的财政状况将进一步恶化。若该政策最终实施，此类压力恐将重现。随后，财务大臣片山宣之发表干预警告，证实日本正与美国就外汇问题进行密切协调，双方均在积极监测市场状况。当日美元兑日元汇率波动有限，徘徊在159.70附近，美元整体走势平淡。整体而言，各市场表现低迷，韩国和日本股市从近期创下的历史高点回落。 在企业方面，Alphabet宣布拟进行800亿美元的股权融资，以扩建人工智能基础设施和计算能力，其中包括300亿美元的承销发行、400亿美元的按市价发行计划，以及来自伯克希尔·哈撒韦的100亿美元私募融资。——澳大利亚现金利率、通胀率以及高于这两者的工资涨幅：本文由investinglive.com的Eamonn Sheridan撰写。

items.title: investingLive European markets wrap: Oil up, equities steady as US-Iran standoff continues
items.summary: Headlines: Iran chief negotiator continues to pinpoint violations of existing ceasefire agreement Iran navy claims that 15 vessels passed through Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours Back from the weekend and still no closer to a US-Iran deal US president Trump says to "sit back and relax, it will all work out well in the end" Gold erases gains as hopes for an imminent US-Iran deal fade. What's next? Long-term consumer inflation expectations remained stable in April according to ECB survey Euro area manufacturing activity loses steam as stockpiling surge fades in May UK May final manufacturing PMI 53.9 vs 53.7 prelim German retail sales struggle in April as Middle East conflict continues to weigh Markets: WTI crude up 3.6% to $90.55 European indices mostly little changed S&P 500 futures up 0.3% USD and GBP lead, NZD lags on the day US 10-year yields up 0.2 bps to 4.46% Gold down 0.6% to $4,506 Bitcoin down 1.3% to $72,614 It's a brand new week but it feels like we are stuck in the same old loop once again. The US and Iran are continuing to sort out terms for a deal to be struck, with both sides unable to find common ground on key issues still. Iran continues to point the finger at the US in saying that there remains a violation with the attacks in Lebanon and the US naval blockade still not being lifted. Officials also continue to lambast the US on frequent changes to the framework agreement terms in the past week. Meanwhile, the US continues to want Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and offer some baseline promises on nuclear arrangements. That said, Trump's latest communique even called to "sit back and relax". That suggests there is no rush to force an agreement for now. As we get into the new week, oil prices are pushing up again with WTI crude moving back above $90 to start the week. Prices are up 3.6% to $90.55, with optimism on a deal being pushed back a bit. However, equities continue to hold out hope for the most part. Major indices in Europe are little changed but US futures are continuing to nudge higher to start the new week/month. S&P 500 futures are up 0.3% with Dow futures up 0.5% while Nasdaq futures are also up 0.3% on the day. In the major currencies space, the dollar is lightly changed and keeping just a little higher so far today. EUR/USD is down 0.1% to 1.1645 with USD/JPY continuing to hover near intervention strike range at 159.50. Meanwhile, USD/CAD is up 0.2% to 1.3830 and AUD/USD down 0.1% to 0.7175 currently. In other markets, bond yields are lightly changed with 10-year Treasury yields sitting at 4.46%. Meanwhile, gold is down 0.6% to $4,506 as the push and pull continues with traders being taken for a ride in this whole deal or no deal saga. This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 头条新闻：伊朗首席谈判代表继续指认违反现有停火协议的行为 伊朗海军称过去24小时内有15艘船只通过霍尔木兹海峡 周末过后，美伊协议仍无进展 美国总统特朗普表示“坐下来放松，最终一切都会好起来的” 随着美伊协议即将达成的希望破灭，黄金回吐涨幅。接下来会怎样？欧洲央行调查显示，4月长期通胀预期保持稳定 5月库存积压势头减弱，欧元区制造业活动动能减弱 英国5月终值制造业PMI为53.9，初值为53.7 中东冲突持续施压，德国4月零售销售表现疲软 市场：WTI原油上涨3.6%至90.55美元 欧洲股指基本持平 标普500指数期货上涨0.3% 美元和英镑领涨，纽元表现落后 美国10年期国债收益率上升0.2个基点至4.46% 黄金下跌0.6%至4,506美元 比特币下跌1.3%至72,614美元 虽然是崭新的一周，但感觉我们似乎又陷入了老一套的循环。美国与伊朗仍在就达成协议的条款进行磋商，双方在关键问题上仍未能找到共同点。 伊朗继续将矛头指向美国，称美国在黎巴嫩发动袭击且尚未解除海上封锁，这仍构成协议违约。伊朗官员还继续抨击美国在过去一周内频繁更改框架协议条款。与此同时，美国仍希望伊朗重新开放霍尔木兹海峡，并在核安排方面作出一些基本承诺。尽管如此，特朗普在最新声明中甚至呼吁“坐下来放松一下”。这表明目前尚无迫切需要强行达成协议。随着新一周的开始，油价再度攀升，西德克萨斯中质原油（WTI）开盘重回90美元上方。油价上涨3.6%至90.55美元，市场对达成协议的乐观预期有所降温。不过，股市整体上仍保持乐观。欧洲主要股指基本持平，但美国期货在新的一周/新的一月伊始继续小幅走高。标普500指数期货上涨0.3%，道指期货上涨0.5%，纳斯达克期货当日也上涨0.3%。在主要货币市场方面，美元汇率波动不大，今日迄今小幅走高。 欧元兑美元下跌0.1%至1.1645，美元兑日元继续徘徊在159.50的干预触发区间附近。与此同时，美元兑加元上涨0.2%至1.3830，澳元兑美元下跌0.1%至0.7175。在其他市场方面，债券收益率变化不大，10年期国债收益率维持在4.46%。与此同时，金价下跌0.6%至4,506美元，随着“有协议”与“无协议”的拉锯战持续，交易员在这场漫长的博弈中被玩弄于股掌之间。本文由investinglive.com的Justin Low撰写。

items.title: Markets continue to stay on edge awaiting US-Iran deal
items.summary: The main talk of the town yesterday was US president Trump looking to broker a ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon. He confirmed that by saying that "all shooting will stop", allowing for the US to at least try and negotiate with Iran again on a broader framework agreement. As mentioned before, the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire is a key precondition that Iran wants as part of the terms for the memorandum of understanding. However, the key question here is whether Israel will abide by that ceasefire. And as we have seen from a few hours ago here , it may not really be the case. If it cannot last a day, how is it expected to carry through for the next 60 days when the US-Iran deal is finalised? As a reminder, these are the other key terms that need to be agreed upon. Otherwise, the whole deal/memorandum of understanding may fall apart at any time once signed. For now, markets are still waiting on the US and Iran to strike a compromise on all fronts. Then, we'll see how things go with nuclear discussions. But as the wait continues, market players are pretty much being put on edge in the meantime. After pushing up yesterday, US futures are now pointing lower with S&P 500 futures down 0.4% on the day. Tech shares are leading declines with Nasdaq futures down 0.6%. The overnight comments by OpenAI CEO, Sam Altman here are perhaps worth looking over if anything else. Besides that, oil prices also posted its best daily showing since late April yesterday with WTI crude nearly touching $95. That fizzled late on and we're seeing a slight pullback now with prices down 1% to $91.20 on the day. In other markets, the US dollar continues to remain little changed in the major currencies space. There's no real conviction here as traders continue to wait on US-Iran developments before really committing to any moves. That being said, USD/JPY continues to hold at 159.70 and nearby intervention strike range so that is one to keep an eye out for. Besides that, bond yields are also being pushed down with 10-year Treasury yields now at 4.44% and 30-year yields at 4.96%. It's a cooling sign but it won't take much to reignite the flames, especially if any deal comes to naught between the US and Iran. And looking to precious metals, gold is back up today by 0.5% to $4,508. The push and pull in gold continues amid a mix of US-Iran optimism and worries about inflation, with the latter signaling a more hawkish outlook for major central banks. After so much action, gold is just down 0.7% when benchmarked to close from the last two weeks i.e. 15 May. This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 昨天城中热议的话题是美国总统特朗普试图在以色列和黎巴嫩之间斡旋停火。他证实了这一点，称“所有交火都将停止”，这使得美国至少能够再次尝试与伊朗就一项更广泛的框架协议进行谈判。如前所述，以色列与真主党之间的停火是伊朗作为谅解备忘录条款所提出的关键前提条件。然而，关键问题在于以色列是否会遵守该停火协议。正如我们几小时前在此所见，情况可能并非如此。如果停火连一天都维持不了，又如何指望在美伊协议最终敲定后的未来60天内持续下去？需要提醒的是，还有其他关键条款亟待达成共识。否则，一旦签署，整份协议或谅解备忘录随时可能破裂。目前，市场仍在等待美伊双方在所有方面达成妥协。届时，我们将看到核谈判的进展。但在等待持续的过程中，市场参与者们已处于高度紧张状态。 在昨日上涨后，美国期货目前呈下跌趋势，标普500指数期货当日下跌0.4%。科技股领跌，纳斯达克期货下跌0.6%。OpenAI首席执行官山姆·阿尔特曼（Sam Altman）昨晚的评论或许值得关注。此外，昨日油价也创下4月下旬以来最大单日涨幅，WTI原油一度逼近95美元。但涨势在尾盘消退，目前价格小幅回调，日跌幅达1%，报91.20美元。其他市场方面，美元兑主要货币汇率基本持平。市场缺乏明确方向，交易员仍在观望美伊局势发展，暂未采取实质行动。不过，美元兑日元仍维持在159.70附近，该水平接近干预触发区间，值得密切关注。此外，债券收益率持续走低，10年期美债收益率目前为4.44%，30年期为4.96%。这虽是市场降温的迹象，但只需一点火花便可能重燃市场热度，尤其是若美伊谈判最终无果而终。贵金属方面，黄金今日回升0.5%，报4,508美元。 在对美伊局势的乐观情绪与通胀担忧交织的背景下，黄金市场持续呈现涨跌互现的态势，其中通胀担忧预示着主要央行将采取更为鹰派的立场。经过此番剧烈波动，若以过去两周（即5月15日）的收盘价为基准，黄金价格仅下跌0.7%。本文作者为investinglive.com的Justin Low。
items.summaryEn: The main talk of the town yesterday was US president Trump looking to broker a ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon. He confirmed that by saying that "all shooting will stop", allowing for the US to at least try and negotiate with Iran again on a broader framework agreement. As mentioned before, the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire is a key precondition that Iran wants as part of the terms for the memorandum of understanding. However, the key question here is whether Israel will abide by that ceasefire. And as we have seen from a few hours ago here , it may not really be the case. If it cannot last a day, how is it expected to carry through for the next 60 days when the US-Iran deal is finalised? As a reminder, these are the other key terms that need to be agreed upon. Otherwise, the whole deal/memorandum of understanding may fall apart at any time once signed. For now, markets are still waiting on the US and Iran to strike a compromise on all fronts. Then, we'll see how things go with nuclear discussions. But as the wait continues, market players are pretty much being put on edge in the meantime. After pushing up yesterday, US futures are now pointing lower with S&P 500 futures down 0.4% on the day. Tech shares are leading declines with Nasdaq futures down 0.6%. The overnight comments by OpenAI CEO, Sam Altman here are perhaps worth looking over if anything else. Besides that, oil prices also posted its best daily showing since late April yesterday with WTI crude nearly touching $95. That fizzled late on and we're seeing a slight pullback now with prices down 1% to $91.20 on the day. In other markets, the US dollar continues to remain little changed in the major currencies space. There's no real conviction here as traders continue to wait on US-Iran developments before really committing to any moves. That being said, USD/JPY continues to hold at 159.70 and nearby intervention strike range so that is one to keep an eye out for. Besides that, bond yields are also being pushed down with 10-year Treasury yields now at 4.44% and 30-year yields at 4.96%. It's a cooling sign but it won't take much to reignite the flames, especially if any deal comes to naught between the US and Iran. And looking to precious metals, gold is back up today by 0.5% to $4,508. The push and pull in gold continues amid a mix of US-Iran optimism and worries about inflation, with the latter signaling a more hawkish outlook for major central banks. After so much action, gold is just down 0.7% when benchmarked to close from the last two weeks i.e. 15 May. This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
